meta.title: Ingredient-level screening contraindications (GFE intake)
meta.scope: Per-INGREDIENT contraindications and cautions, mapped to the FORM-IV-GFE-01 Section-B screening conditions, allergy (Section C) and medication (Section D) screens. Deliberately NOT coupled to any protocol/stack — a contraindication attaches to the ingredient itself, so it fires no matter which stack or a-la-carte build the ingredient lands in.
meta.how_it_links: GFE screens the condition (e.g. 'Kidney disease / dialysis'). When a patient with that flag gets a build that contains an ingredient whose screen lists that flag, the bench raises the matching alert (avoid = hard stop in MD-of-record mode / red advisory otherwise; caution/screen = amber).

levels.avoid: Contraindicated for this condition at wellness IV/IM dosing — do not give without provider override + documented rationale.
levels.caution: Use with provider judgment / dose adjustment / slower rate.
levels.screen: Not a contraindication per se — requires a specific check (lab, medication reconciliation, allergy history) before giving.

meta.honesty_notes: Items marked VERIFY are mechanistically plausible but rest on limited or contested human data — the MD must confirm before they are treated as hard rules. Everything else is established pharmacology. No dose, citation, or interaction below is invented; where the locked iv-module.json already carries a guardrail it is referenced, not duplicated.
meta.published_by: Armando A. Falcon, MD — AllyOS curator (editorial review; not a prescription)

screen.note: Sodium + volume load — slow rate / smaller bag in CHF or significant cardiac disease to avoid fluid overload.
Volume + sodium caution in advanced renal failure / oliguria.
Large sodium load can transiently raise BP in poorly controlled hypertension.
Contains potassium (~4 mEq/L) — avoid as a large-volume base in hyperkalemia or dialysis-dependent ESRD.
Lactate is hepatically metabolized — caution in severe liver failure.
Volume load in CHF.
Contains thiamine — IV thiamine can rarely cause anaphylaxis; screen prior B-vitamin/infusion reactions.
Cobalt/cobalamin hypersensitivity (rare).
Polycythemia vera and some myeloproliferative states can be aggravated by B12 — screen if relevant hematologic history. VERIFY relevance per patient.
High-dose IV Vitamin C can precipitate acute hemolysis in G6PD deficiency — screen BEFORE any dose >2 g (already wired in bench engine; CARD-VC tiering ≥6 g).
Oxalate is a Vit C metabolite — high-dose IV risks oxalate nephropathy; avoid high dose in CKD/ESRD/dialysis.
History of calcium-oxalate kidney stones — limit dose / hydrate.
Iron overload / hemochromatosis: Vit C increases iron absorption/mobilization — screen for iron-overload states.

High-dose biotin interferes with many streptavidin-biotin lab immunoassays (troponin, TSH/free-T4, hCG, others) — can cause falsely high or low results. Hold before labs / inform the lab. Not a true contraindication.
Magnesium is renally cleared — accumulation/toxicity in renal impairment or dialysis; avoid or markedly reduce + slow rate.
Heart block / significant bradycardia — IV Mg can worsen AV conduction; screen for conduction disease.
Myasthenia gravis and neuromuscular blockers — Mg potentiates weakness. VERIFY MG history.
Digoxin / cardiac glycosides — IV calcium can precipitate fatal arrhythmia ('stone heart'); screen the med list.
Malignancy with hypercalcemia (bone mets, PTHrP) — avoid adding calcium.
Stones / hypercalcemia risk; BMP above ceiling per module.
Reduce in significant renal impairment (trace-element accumulation).
Trace-element accumulation in renal failure — keep at/under ceiling.
Manganese is biliary-excreted and accumulates in cholestasis / liver disease (neurotoxic, basal-ganglia deposition) — avoid the Mn-containing blend in cholestatic liver disease.
Trace-element accumulation in renal failure.
Amino-acid load — caution in significant renal impairment.
L-Carnitine antagonizes thyroid hormone action peripherally — caution / avoid in hypothyroidism; can blunt thyroid replacement. VERIFY thyroid status.
Accumulates in ESRD (though used therapeutically in dialysis) — provider judgment.
May potentiate warfarin (INR rise) — screen anticoagulants. VERIFY.
Glutamine is metabolized to ammonia/glutamate — avoid in cirrhosis / hepatic encephalopathy or severe liver disease.
Nitrogen load in advanced renal disease.
Contains ARGININE — the HCl salt shifts potassium extracellularly and has caused fatal hyperkalemia in renal impairment (see ING_ARGININE).
Amino-acid/nitrogen load in advanced liver disease.
Arginine may favor HSV reactivation in patients with frequent oral/genital herpes — counsel/screen. VERIFY relevance per patient.
Sulfur-containing — rare bronchospasm reports; caution in brittle asthma. IV PUSH ONLY / never in a Vit C bag (compatibility, per module).
Sulfite/sulfur sensitivity.
Rapid infusion causes chest tightness, palpitations, flushing, nausea — strictly rate-limited (rate floors in module); extra caution with cardiac disease.
Weight-loss/lipotropic agents — avoid in pregnancy/breastfeeding.
Methionine/choline load — caution in significant liver disease.
Caution in significant renal impairment.
Can provoke bronchospasm — caution in active asthma.
Prior NAC reaction (anaphylactoid).
CoQ10 can lower INR / antagonize warfarin — screen anticoagulant use and monitor.
Same warfarin interaction noted in Section D reconciliation.
Leber's hereditary optic neuropathy and tobacco/nutritional amblyopia — cyanocobalamin is contraindicated (cyanide moiety); use methyl- or hydroxocobalamin. Also cobalt allergy.
Cyanide moiety accumulates in severe renal failure — prefer hydroxo/methyl form.
Can transiently raise blood pressure; expected reddish discoloration of urine/skin.
Cobalt/cobalamin hypersensitivity (rare).
Dexpanthenol may prolong bleeding time — caution in hemophilia / on anticoagulants. VERIFY.
Levodopa WITHOUT carbidopa — B6 accelerates peripheral levodopa metabolism and reduces efficacy; screen Parkinson's meds.
Chronic high-dose B6 causes sensory peripheral neuropathy — respect the 100 mg ceiling and avoid stacking other B6 sources.
Granulomatous disease / sarcoidosis / some lymphomas convert vitamin D and cause hypercalcemia — screen.
Hypercalcemia/hypercalciuria risk — caution with calcium stones.

Insulin Autoimmune Syndrome (Hirata) trigger AND additive hypoglycemia with insulin/sulfonylureas — screen diabetes meds and prior reaction (module carries the full Hirata guardrail). Give with thiamine repletion.
May alter thyroid hormone levels — caution in thyroid disease. VERIFY.
Digoxin / cardiac glycosides — fatal-arrhythmia risk (as with Ca gluconate). Vesicant: high extravasation injury; gluconate preferred peripherally (module).
Hypercalcemia of malignancy.
HCl salt shifts potassium extracellularly — has caused FATAL hyperkalemia in renal impairment; screen renal function (module guardrail).
Post-MI use was associated with harm (VINTAGE trial) — caution in recent MI; vasodilatory hypotension.
May favor HSV reactivation — counsel patients with frequent herpes. VERIFY relevance.
Amino-acid/nitrogen load — caution in hepatic encephalopathy.
Trace-element accumulation in renal failure; do not double-dose with standalone minerals (module).
If Mn-containing — cholestasis accumulation (see Trace).
Contains Vitamin C — inherits the G6PD hemolysis screen.
Inherits Vit C oxalate/renal caution.
Contains glutathione — inherits the asthma/bronchospasm caution.
Lipotropic/weight-loss family — avoid in pregnancy/breastfeeding.
Methionine/choline load.
Caution in significant renal impairment.

Potent MAO-A inhibitor — serotonin-syndrome risk with SSRIs/SNRIs/TCAs, tramadol/meperidine/fentanyl/methadone, dextromethorphan, bupropion; screen FULL med list incl. ~2-week washout (module guardrail).
Hemolysis risk in G6PD deficiency.
Avoid in pregnancy.
Caution in renal impairment; falsely lowers SpO2 reading; blue-green urine expected.
Dose-dependent QT prolongation — screen congenital long-QT and other QT-prolonging meds; caution with significant cardiac disease / electrolyte derangement.
Additive QT risk with other QT-prolonging drugs; serotonergic load (rare).
NSAID — platelet inhibition + GI/bleeding risk; avoid with anticoagulants/bleeding disorder.
Nephrotoxic — avoid in renal impairment / volume depletion.
Bleeding risk around recent surgery/procedure.
NSAID-exacerbated respiratory disease (aspirin-sensitive asthma/nasal polyps).
Avoid, especially 3rd trimester (ductal closure).
Fluid retention / BP elevation; caution in CHF/HTN.
Renally cleared — reduce dose (CrCl <50); CNS effects/confusion in elderly; give slow/diluted (rare QT in renal impairment) (module guardrail).

ING_NIAGEN_NR.name: Niagen / Nicotinamide Riboside (NR) — NAD+ PRECURSOR (NOT in current inventory; NOT IV NAD+)
ING_NIAGEN_NR.distinct_from: ING_NAD — NR is an oral/IV NAD+ precursor, a DIFFERENT molecule from infused NAD+; no proven peptide synergy (direct_combination_evidence:false in module market_additions).

screen.note: Insufficient human safety data in pregnancy/lactation — avoid.
NAD+ precursors raise cellular NAD+; theoretical concern about fueling some tumors — screen active malignancy and defer to oncology. VERIFY — limited/contested human data.
Reconcile other NAD+ precursors / niacin-family supplements to avoid stacking; flushing uncommon with NR.

ING_NIAGEN_NR.source_audit: Standing-order draft (protocols/niagen-nr-iv-standing-order.md) carries the verified citations (Conze Sci Rep 2019; Hawkins medRxiv 2024; Reyna Front Aging 2026). Injectable NR sourcing is the clinic/provider's decision (pharmacy-agnostic, advisory) — AllyOS does not mandate a source. Requires a prescriber-signed standing order before clinical use.
